metadata.website_type: academic research organization and conferences site

tasks.name: Bookmark an affordable AI conference in Europe for late 2025
tasks.description: Find an international AI-related conference in Europe happening between September and December 2025 with a student registration fee under $250 and add it to your bookmarks list.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click on the main navigation menu item labeled "Conferences".
On the conferences page, locate and click the filter or dropdown for "Region" and select "Europe".
Find the date range filter and set the start date to "September 1, 2025" and the end date to "December 31, 2025".
Locate the topic or track filter, type "artificial intelligence" into the topic field, and select the matching AI-related topic from the suggestions.
Find the registration fee filter, set the attendee type to "Student", and enter "250" as the maximum fee in the price field.
Click the sort dropdown for the conference list and choose the option "Start Date: Soonest First".
From the refreshed list, click the title of the first conference that matches the filters to open its detail page.
On the conference detail page, click the button or icon labeled "Add to bookmarks" or "Save conference".

tasks.name: Create a one-day schedule with three climate sessions across multiple tracks
tasks.description: Within a selected conference, build a personal schedule for a specific day by adding 3 sessions related to climate change that occur between 9:00 and 17:00, ensuring they cover at least 2 different tracks.
steps: From the homepage, click the "Conferences" menu item and then click on any upcoming conference title to open its detail page.
On the conference detail page, click the tab or link labeled "Program", "Schedule", or similar.
Use the day selector or calendar control to select a single full conference day (for example, the second day listed).
In the search or filter box within the schedule, type "climate" and apply the filter to show climate-related sessions.
Use any available time filter or manually scan the list to identify sessions that start at or after 09:00 and end by 17:00.
From the filtered sessions, click the first session that meets the time criteria and click the button labeled "Add to My Schedule" or similar.
From the remaining climate-related sessions on that day, choose a second session from a different track or theme (based on the track label shown) and add it to your schedule using the same button.
Select a third climate-related session that still falls between 09:00 and 17:00 and, if possible, belongs to either the first or another distinct track, then add it to your schedule.
Click on the "My Schedule" or "Personal Agenda" tab or link to view the combined list of the 3 added sessions.

tasks.name: Follow a mid-career researcher in machine learning for healthcare from Canada
tasks.description: Find a researcher whose expertise is machine learning for healthcare, based in Canada, with an h-index between 30 and 60, and add them to your followed researchers list.
steps: From the homepage, click on the navigation menu item labeled "Researchers" or "People".
Locate the search bar on the researchers page and type "machine learning healthcare" into the search field, then start the search.
On the results page, open the filters panel and select "Canada" from the country or affiliation location filter.
Find the metric or h-index filter and enter "30" as the minimum value and "60" as the maximum value, then apply the filter.
If available, use the expertise or keywords filter and select any option that closely matches "machine learning for healthcare".
Scroll through the filtered list and click on the name of the first researcher whose profile snippet mentions healthcare-related machine learning topics to open their profile page.
On the researcher’s profile page, locate and click the button labeled "Follow", "Add to followed researchers", or similar.
Navigate to the "My Account" or "Followed Researchers" section from the top navigation or user menu to open the list of followed researchers.

tasks.name: Add three recent open-access renewable energy policy papers to a reading list
tasks.description: Search for open-access publications on renewable energy policy from 2021–2024 in journals with an impact factor above 5, then add the first three matching results to your reading list.
steps: From the homepage, click on the "Publications" or "Research" menu item.
In the publications search bar, type "renewable energy policy" and submit the search.
Locate the publication year filter and set the start year to "2021" and the end year to "2024".
Find the access type filter and select the checkbox or option labeled "Open Access".
Locate the journal impact factor filter and set the minimum impact factor value to "5" before applying the filter.
Click the sort dropdown for the results and choose "Most Recent First" or a similar recency-based sorting option.
From the filtered and sorted results, click on the title of the first publication to open its detail page.
On the publication detail page, click the button labeled "Add to reading list" or "Save to library".
Use the back navigation or a "Back to results" link to return to the search results, then repeat the process of opening the next two publication titles and adding each one to your reading list.
After adding three items, click on the "My Reading List" or "Saved Publications" link in the header or user menu to open the list of saved publications.

tasks.name: Pre-register as a guest for an affordable half-day data visualization workshop
tasks.description: Find a half-day workshop on data visualization with a fee under $150, choose the morning option if multiple time slots are offered, and complete the pre-registration form using guest registration without making a payment.
steps: From the homepage, click on the "Events" or "Workshops" menu item.
In the events search bar, type "data visualization" and start the search.
Use the event type filter to select "Workshop" and apply the filter.
Locate the duration filter and choose the option labeled "Half-day" or similar, then apply it.
Find the fee or price filter, set the maximum fee to "150" (in US dollars if a currency is required), and apply the filter.
From the filtered list, click on the title of the first data visualization workshop to open its detail page.
On the workshop detail page, click the "Register" or "Pre-register" button, then choose the "Guest" or "Register without account" option if prompted.
If multiple time slots are shown, select the morning session (for example, the one that starts earlier in the day) from the available options.

Fill out the registration form fields with sample data: enter "Alex Guest" as the full name, "alex.guest@example.com" as the email address, and "Independent" as the affiliation, then choose any available payment option that indicates "Pay on-site" or "Invoice later".
Click the button at the bottom of the form labeled "Submit registration" or "Complete pre-registration" to finish the guest registration process.

tasks.name: Subscribe to weekly AI ethics and climate policy newsletters with HTML format
tasks.description: Use the newsletter preferences page to subscribe to both the "AI Ethics" and "Climate Policy" newsletters with a weekly frequency using the same email address, and configure the delivery format as HTML.
steps: From the homepage, scroll to the footer or open the main menu and click on the link labeled "Newsletters" or "Email Updates".
On the newsletter subscriptions page, find the email address field and enter "research.subscriber@example.com".
In the list of available newsletter topics, select the checkbox or toggle for "AI Ethics".
In the same list, also select the checkbox or toggle for "Climate Policy".
Locate the frequency dropdown or radio buttons for each selected newsletter and choose "Weekly" for both "AI Ethics" and "Climate Policy".
Find the email format setting, and select the option labeled "HTML" (instead of "Plain text" or other formats).
If there is a general preferences section, leave all other options at their defaults and click the "Save preferences" or "Subscribe" button to submit the form.

tasks.name: Make an anonymous $75 donation to the Early Career Research Grants program
tasks.description: Use the donations page to make a one-time $75 donation allocated fully to the 'Early Career Research Grants' program, selecting anonymous donation and opting out of physical mail.
steps: From the homepage, click on the navigation link labeled "Support Us", "Donate", or similar.
On the donations page, select the "One-time" donation option instead of recurring options.
In the amount field, type "75" as the donation amount in US dollars.
Locate the fund or program dropdown and select "Early Career Research Grants" from the list of available funds.
Find and select the checkbox or radio button labeled "Make this donation anonymous" or similar.
In the communication preferences section, select the option indicating that you do not wish to receive physical mail (for example, "Email only" or "No postal mail").

Fill in the required donor information fields with sample data: enter "Jordan Donor" as the name and "jordan.donor@example.com" as the email address, leaving any optional address fields blank if allowed.
Proceed to the next step by clicking the button labeled "Continue" or "Review donation", stopping before any payment information is entered.

tasks.name: Choose the most economical membership plan that still includes key benefits
tasks.description: Compare the Student and Professional membership plans, choose the plan with the lower annual cost that still includes both 'access to member-only webinars' and 'discounted conference fees', and begin the application by completing the initial form step.
steps: From the homepage, click on the navigation menu item labeled "Membership" or "Join".
On the membership overview page, locate the section that compares membership plans and click on "Student" to view its details in a comparison view.
In the same comparison area, also select or display the "Professional" plan so that both Student and Professional benefits and prices are visible.

Compare the annual prices and benefit lists, and identify which of the two plans has the lower annual cost while still listing both "access to member-only webinars" and "discounted conference fees" as included benefits.
Click the "Apply" or "Join" button associated with the plan that meets these criteria (lower annual cost with both specified benefits).
On the membership application form that appears, enter "Taylor Member" as the full name in the name field.
Fill in the email field with "taylor.member@example.com" and select a country such as "United States" from the country dropdown.

If a membership eligibility or role field is present (such as "Occupation" or "Status"), choose the option that best fits the selected plan (for example, "Student" for Student plan or "Professional" for Professional plan).
Click the button labeled "Continue", "Next", or "Save and proceed" to advance to the next application step, stopping before any payment or billing information is entered.
